behavior_summary: PALEGG::hatch is the U8P_PALEGG::hatch-event handler for a PALEGG object. When the U8P_PALEGG::hatch event fires it first checks the global rule flag 'devonInRule'; if that flag is false the handler returns immediately and does nothing.

If 'devonInRule' is true the handler sets an info/state on the target object (pushes info id 0x0210 and the event parameter), excludes that object from normal processing, computes a future time via TimeInMinutes(pid, 7), and calls Npc::schedule(time, handler_address) to schedule a future NPC/event action.
After scheduling it performs an implies/pop style operation using the last return value, stores the result into a temporary local, and then suspends the process (yields until resumed by the engine).
notable_control_flow: Entry is gated by the global flag 'devonInRule' — false = early return, true = schedule path.

Deterministic sequence in the true-path: set info (0x0210) on parameter, process-exclude the target, call TimeInMinutes, then call Npc::schedule with the computed time and a handler address, then perform an 'implies' result handling and suspend.
The function runs as a process-style handler (it uses suspend/yield semantics rather than a one-shot immediate action).
open_questions: What semantic meaning does the literal '7' passed to TimeInMinutes have (minutes delay, time slot index, or predefined schedule ID)?
What exactly is the handler/address taken from the stack ([SP+04h]) that is passed to Npc::schedule (a callback, script address, or NPC id)?
What precise effect does the 'implies' operation have here — is it purely an internal flow control artifact or does it modify engine state?
What object/context is referenced by the BP+06h parameter when used with 'set info' (the egg itself, a related NPC, or another entity)?

main_function.name: U8P_PALEGG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Handle the U8P_PALEGG::hatch event for a PALEGG object: conditionally schedule a future NPC/event action and then suspend, gated by a global rule.
entry_conditions: Event 7 (U8P_PALEGG::hatch) invoked for the PALEGG instance
Global flag 'devonInRule' must be true for the scheduling path to run

function_map.name: U8P_PALEGG::hatch
function_map.role: U8P_PALEGG::hatch event handler for PALEGG that conditionally schedules an NPC/event and suspends

function_map.name: U8P_PALEGG::TimeInMinutes
function_map.role: compute/return a time value used for scheduling (called with pid and literal 7)

function_map.name: Npc::schedule
function_map.role: schedule an NPC or event to occur at the given time with the provided handler/address

function_map.name: U8P_PALEGG::set info
function_map.role: engine operation to set an info/state key (0x0210) on an object (inferred engine call)

function_map.name: U8P_PALEGG::process exclude
function_map.role: engine operation to exclude the target object from normal processing/AI (inferred)

external_calls.purpose: Set info id 0x0210 on the object/context supplied by BP+06h before scheduling (inferred preparatory state change)
Exclude the referenced object from further processing (inferred protective/preventative action)
Compute a scheduling time value (called with the current pid and literal 7); its return value is used as the time parameter to Npc::schedule
Schedule an NPC/event to run at the computed time using a handler/address from the stack ([SP+04h])

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Acts as a gate controlling whether Devon-related rules/actions are active; when false the U8P_PALEGG::hatch handler returns immediately

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: PALEGG::hatch is the U8P_PALEGG::hatch-event handler for a PALEGG object. When the U8P_PALEGG::hatch event fires it first checks the global rule flag 'devonInRule'; if that flag is false the handler returns immediately and does nothing.
